data.title: Ex-Goldman banker Leissner gets two years in prison in 1MDB fraud case
data.description:
(May 30): Former Goldman Sachs Group Inc banker Tim Leissner, who pleaded guilty to helping loot the Malaysian investment fund 1MDB, was sentenced to two years in prison over his role in the massive fraud.

US district judge Margo Brodie in Brooklyn, New York, sentenced him at a hearing Thursday. Leissner had asked not to go to prison, arguing that his cooperation had resulted in the conviction of a former colleague and billions of dollars in global fines against Goldman.

Leissner, 53, had faced as long as 25 years in prison after pleading guilty in 2018 to one count of conspiring to violate bribery laws and conspiring to launder money. While prosecutors had also pushed for leniency, Brodie cited a letter from Goldman that said that the 1MDB fraud would never have taken place without Leissner.

“Your brazen and audacious conduct meant you were in a position to provide substantial assistance to the government,” Brodie said. “But in the end, your cooperation does not completely make up for the harm and devastation you knowingly caused through your conduct which was completely selfish. So some punishment is warranted.”

The German citizen was at the centre of what US prosecutors described as one of the largest financial frauds in history in which billions of dollars were siphoned from the Malaysian investment fund, 1MDB. Additionally, the US said more than US$1 billion (RM4.2 billion) was diverted from bond proceeds to pay bribes paid to officials in Malaysia and Abu Dhabi.

But Leissner cooperated with the US and testified as the government’s star witness against his former Goldman colleague Roger Ng at a 2022 trial. Leissner spent 10 days on the witness stand describing the sprawling fraud. Ng was convicted and sentenced to 10 years in prison and sent home to Malaysia to face prosecution there.

The fraud involved staggering sums. A forensic accountant with the FBI who traced diverted funds testified in 2022 that former Malaysian prime minister Datuk Seri Najib Razak collected at least US$756 million, Leissner got US$73.4 million, Ng took US$35.1 million and fugitive Malaysian financier Jho Low got US$1.42 billion.

At least US$60 million was used to help produce the 2013 Martin Scorsese film The Wolf of Wall Street. The US Justice Department has recovered at least US$1.4 billion in assess, including a boutique Beverly Hills hotel, fine art by Monet, Picasso and Van Gogh.

Uploaded by Magessan Varatharaja

data.title: UN may cut staff by 20%, internal memo says
data.description:
WASHINGTON (May 29): The United Nations Secretariat is preparing to cut its $3.7 billion budget by 20% and slash about 6,900 jobs, according to an internal memo seen by Reuters.

The directive, which asks staff to detail cuts by June 13, comes amid a financial crisis triggered in part by the United States, which annually funds nearly a quarter of the world body.

In addition to U.S. foreign aid cuts under President Donald Trump that have gutted U.N. humanitarian agencies, the U.S. owes - for arrears and the current fiscal year - nearly $1.5 billion.

The memo's author, U.N. Controller Chandramouli Ramanathan, did not cite the U.S. failure to pay. He noted that the cuts are part of a review launched in March dubbed "UN80."

"It is an ambitious effort to ensure that the United Nations is fit for purpose to support 21st-century multilateralism, reduce human suffering and build better lives and futures for all," Ramanathan said. "I count on your cooperation for this collective effort whose aggressive timelines are recognized."

The cuts would take effect January 1, the start of the next budget cycle.

In public briefings to U.N. diplomats this month, Secretary General Antonio Guterres said he is considering a massive overhaul that would merge major departments and shift resources across the globe. He said the U.N. may consolidate some agencies, trim others, move staff to cheaper cities, cut duplication and eliminate redundant bureaucracy.

"These are times of peril, but they are also times of profound opportunity and obligation," Guterres said on May 12.

"Make no mistake: uncomfortable and difficult decisions lie ahead. It may be easier and even tempting to ignore them or kick the can down the roads. But that road is a dead end."

The U.S. failure to pay its assessments has also created a liquidity crisis for the U.N., a problem exacerbated by China’s repeated late payments. Together, the two nations account for more than 40% of U.N. funding.

In addition, the Trump administration has withdrawn hundreds of millions of dollars in discretionary funds, forcing the abrupt halt of dozens of humanitarian programs that U.N. officials have said will cost lives. The proposed U.S. budget for the coming year, which must be approved by Congress, has eliminated or drastically reduced funding for several U.N. programs, including peacekeeping.

In April, Tom Fletcher, who leads the United Nations Office for the Coordination of Humanitarian Affairs, told employees that U.S. funding cuts would force the agency to trim 20% of its staff to address a shortfall of $58 million.

Richard Gowan, UN director at the International Crisis Group, said it was unclear if the cuts will affect the Trump administration position.

“Diplomats think that Guterres hopes that if he shows he will make these cuts, then the administration will ease off on their threats to zero out funding for the UN,” Gowan said.

“That's possible. It's also possible that the administration will just pocket the cuts and not make any concessions.”

Uploaded by Siow Chen Ming

data.title: SunCon, IHH, KPJ, Genting, GenM, Chin Hin, Tropicana, Farm Fresh, IJM, Carlsberg Malaysia, NationGate, DNeX
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (May 30): Here is a brief recap of some business news and corporate announcements that made the headlines on Thursday:

Sunway Construction Group Bhd (KL:SUNCON) has secured two work orders valued at RM1.16 billion to undertake general contractor services for two data centre projects awarded by a US-based technology company. The projects are scheduled to begin immediately, with final completion targeted for February 2027. SunCon has secured RM3.5 billion in new orders so far this year, representing over half of its 2025 order book replenishment target range of RM4.5 billion to RM6 billion. Consequently, its total outstanding order book has increased to RM7.9 billion. — SunCon bags two data centre contracts worth RM1.16 bil from US-based tech firm

IHH Healthcare Bhd (KL:IHH) saw its net profit for the first quarter ended March 31, 2025 (1QFY2025) drop 33% to RM514 million from RM768 million a year earlier, primarily due to a lower net monetary gain resulting from accounting rules under MFRS129, as well as the absence of last year’s deferred tax credit from asset revaluation in Türkiye. Revenue for the quarter, however, grew 5.7% to RM6.29 billion from RM5.96 billion a year earlier, driven by stronger contributions from its operations in Malaysia, Türkiye and Europe, supported by improved inpatient volumes. IHH Healthcare did not declare any dividend for the quarter. — IHH Healthcare’s 1Q net profit down 33% due to exceptional accounting adjustments

Private hospital operator KPJ Healthcare Bhd’s (KL:KPJ) net profit fell 20% year-on-year (y-o-y) in the first quarter of 2025, dragged by losses in its overseas operations, coupled with the absence of discontinued operations’ contribution in Australia. Net profit for the three months ended March 31, 2025 (1QFY2025) was RM57.06 million, against RM71.39 million in the same quarter last year, the group said in a filing on Thursday. Revenue for the quarter, meanwhile, rose 7% to RM971.8 million from RM908.02 million, supported by increased patient visits and bed capacity at its Malaysian hospitals. KPJ declared an interim dividend of 0.8 sen per share, amounting to RM34.9 million. — KPJ's 1Q profit falls 20% on losses overseas, pays another dividend

Genting Bhd’s (KL:GENTING) net profit shrank to only RM4.57 million for the first quarter ended March 31, 2025 (1QFY2025) from RM588.87 million a year ago. The sharp fall in net profit was partly due to lower quarterly revenue, which fell 12.4% to RM6.51 billion from RM7.43 billion in 1QFY2024. The group’s hospitality segment generated a lower contribution of RM5.34 billion in the quarter under review compared with RM6.48 billion a year ago. Meanwhile, the group’s other income halved to RM257 million versus RM532 million a year ago. Losses from its share of results in joint ventures and associates also doubled to RM28 million. No dividend was declared for the quarter. — Genting's 1Q net profit down sharply amid lower revenues from casinos and higher taxation

Genting Malaysia Bhd's (KL:GENM) net profit rose 25.69% y-o-y in the first quarter ended March 31, 2025 on the back of foreign exchange gains and improved cost management. The group, which fell into the red in the fourth quarter last year, saw net profit rise to RM72.58 million from RM57.78 million a year earlier. Quarterly revenue slipped 6.14% y-o-y to RM2.6 billion from RM2.76 billion due to weaker contributions from its leisure and hospitality segments in Malaysia and the UK, as well as currency translation effects. No dividend was announced for the quarter. — Forex gain lifts Genting Malaysia's 1Q profit despite revenue slip

Chin Hin Group Bhd (KL:CHINHIN) saw its first-quarter net profit double, mainly on contributions from kitchen and wardrobe maker Signature International Bhd (KL:SIGN) and a turnaround in its loss-making property development segment. Net profit for the quarter under review rose to RM18.39 million from RM9.07 million in the same period a year earlier. Revenue for the quarter under review was 66.9% higher at RM951.95 million compared to RM570.21 million in 1QFY2024. No dividend was declared for the quarter. — Chin Hin’s 1Q net profit doubles on contribution from Signature International, turnaround in property development

Tropicana Corp Bhd (KL:TROP) remained profitable for two consecutive quarters. The property developer posted a net profit of RM1.32 million in the first quarter ended March 31, 2025 (1QFY2025), lifted by a tax credit of RM6.63 million. Quarterly revenue, however, fell 10.61% y-o-y to RM260.36 million from RM291.28 million, attributed to lower recurring income following the completion of several investment property disposals. No dividend was declared for 1QFY2025. — Tropicana reports second consecutive profitable quarter in 1QFY2025

A slew of new products from ice cream to butter and lower input cost lifted Farm Fresh Bhd’s (KL:FFB) net profit to a new record high of RM27.65 million in the fourth quarter ended March 31, 2025 (4QFY2025), a 15% increase when compared to the same quarter in 2024. Revenue for the quarter rose 21% y-o-y to RM170.8 million. For the entire 12 months, net profit surged 68% to RM106.4 million, thanks to full period contribution from Inside Scoop, acquired in May 2023, and Sin Wah, acquired in November 2023. Revenue, meanwhile, climbed 21% to RM981.18 million. No dividend was declared for the quarter. The company had earlier paid out dividends totalling 2.20 sen per share for FY2025. — Farm Fresh logs record-high profits with new products, to speed up Taiping expansion

IJM Corp Bhd’s (KL:IJM) net profit for the fourth quarter was down almost 30% to RM128.95 million, mainly due to a RM54 million expected credit loss related to the West Coast Expressway. Last year’s profits included one-time gains, including a RM67 million fair value gain from WCE Holdings Bhd warrants. Revenue for the quarter ended March 31, 2025 (4QFY2025) was marginally higher at RM1.8 billion. The company declared an interim dividend of five sen and a one sen special dividend, bringing the total payout to eight sen per share for the year. For the full year ended March 31, 2025, net profit dropped 32% to RM403.4 million from RM600.3 million, while revenue increased to RM6.3 billion from RM5.9 billion. — IJM Corp’s 4Q net profit falls almost 30% on credit loss from West Coast Expressway

Carlsberg Brewery Malaysia Bhd (KL:CARLSBG) reported a 7.49% increase in net profit for the first quarter ended March 31, 2025 (1QFY2025), largely due to the absence of a deferred tax liability recognised in the same period last year relating to foreign withholding tax in its Sri Lankan associate, Lion Brewery (Ceylon) PLC (LBCP). The group posted a net profit of RM94.52 million for the quarter, up from RM87.93 million a year earlier. The company declared a first interim single-tier dividend of 23 sen per share for the quarter under review, with payment scheduled for July 28. Despite the profit growth, quarterly revenue declined 8.67% y-o-y to RM662.81 million from RM725.76 million. — Carlsberg Malaysia’s 1Q profit rises 7% on lower taxes, declares 23 sen dividend

NationGate Holdings Bhd (KL:NATGATE) saw its net profit more than double in the first quarter, lifted by strong demand from its data computing segment, coupled with foreign exchange gains of RM56.64 million. Net profit for the three months ended March 31, 2025 (1QFY2025) came in at RM53.9 million compared with RM21 million a year earlier, as revenue surged nearly 14 times to RM2.84 billion from RM207 million. NationGate declared an interim dividend of 0.25 sen per share, unchanged from 1QFY2024. — NationGate 1Q profit more than doubles on strong data segment, declares 0.25 sen dividend

Dagang NeXchange Bhd (KL:DNEX) posted a net loss of RM79.04 million for the first quarter compared with a net profit of RM14.46 million a year earlier, mainly due to a higher interest expense. The company's quarterly revenue fell 4.19% to RM296.83 million from RM309.82 million in 1QFY2024, mainly due to weaker contributions from the energy and information technology segments, which offset gains in the technology segment. DNeX did not declare any dividend for 1QFY2025. — Dagang NeXchange swings to loss in 1Q on higher interest expense

Edited ByS Kanagaraju

data.title: PeterLabs rejects request to convene EGM to remove directors
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (May 30): PeterLabs Holdings Bhd (KL:PLABS) on Thursday said the group has decided not to convene an extraordinary general meeting (EGM) to remove its directors as requested by two substantial shareholders.

“The board is of the view that the resolutions proposed by Bu Yaw Seng and Datin Lin Ching Yein, if passed, would not be in the interest of PeterLabs,” the animal health and nutrition products group said in a bourse filing. “As such, the board has decided not to convene the EGM as requested.”

Bu and Lin, who collectively hold more than a 10% stake in PeterLabs, sought the EGM to remove seven directors and replace them with two new nominees.

The company, in its filing, added that it has initiated a lawsuit against the two shareholders in the High Court in Seremban, seeking to invalidate their request for the EGM and prevent any steps towards its convening.

The company is seeking a court declaration that the requisition notice is invalid and that the proposed resolutions would be detrimental to its interests.

PeterLabs is also requesting an order to restrain Bu and Lin from calling or conducting the EGM.

The call for the EGM came after the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) raided PeterLabs’ principal office and its wholly-owned subsidiary Thye On Tong Trading Sdn Bhd's premises, following a formal complaint lodged by the group regarding allegations against a director, Datuk Loh Saw Foong.

PeterLabs has suspended Loh from his executive duties, pending the outcome of both its internal investigation and an ongoing probe by the MACC.

Shares in PeterLabs closed up two sen or 5.19% to 40.5 sen on Thursday, valuing the group at RM110.03 million. Year to date, the stock has more than doubled, gaining over 100%.

Read also:
PeterLabs shareholders call for EGM to remove seven directors amid internal probe
PeterLabs extends executive director’s suspension pending MACC probe

Edited ByS Kanagaraju

data.title: Federal International Holdings wins earthwork contract in PJ
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (May 29): Federal International Holdings Bhd (KL:FIHB) has secured a contract valued at RM64.89 million for the provision of earthwork, piling and substructure works for a proposed development in Petaling Jaya.

The related-party contract was awarded by Perumahan Masteron Sdn Bhd, said FIHB in a filing with Bursa Malaysia on Thursday.

The development, located at PJ Selatan Sentral, comprises three phases, with Phase 3A set to begin on June 16 and projected to conclude by Aug 15, 2026. 

Phase 3B is expected to be completed by June 15, 2027, while Phase 3C is scheduled to commence on Jan 16, 2027, with completion targeted for Oct 15, 2027.

FIHB expects the project to have a positive impact on the group’s revenue and earnings for the financial year ending June 30, 2026, and throughout the contract period.

The group said several members of the Choy family — including Datuk Dr Choy Fook On, Datin Tan Geok Foong, Datuk Choy Wai Hin, Datuk Choy Wai Ceong, Choy Kin Mann, Choy Yuin Yi and Dr Choy Sook Kuen — are deemed to be interested in the project due to their directorships or shareholdings in FIHB and their connections to Perumahan Masteron Sdn Bhd, which is part of property developer Masteron Group.

FIHB shares closed unchanged at 34 sen on Thursday, valuing the group at RM49 million.

Edited ByS Kanagaraju

data.title: Boeing aims to review move to 47 MAX jets a month near year end
data.description:
(May 29): Boeing Co aims to begin working towards boosting output of its 737 family of jetliners to 47 per month by later this year, a move that would mark a significant improvement in its factories and help put its finances on firmer footing.

The planemaker is quickly approaching producing the cash cow jet at a rate of 38 planes per month, the maximum allowed by US regulators, chief executive officer Kelly Ortberg said on Thursday. That’s the first milestone towards returning the narrowbody jet to a pre-Covid manufacturing pace.

The company plans to then shift to 42 planes a month, provided the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) approves and its supply chains and factories are stable, Ortberg said. Boeing later plans to expand further to 47 jets a month, following a review of milestones that could begin near the end of the 2025, he said in remarks at a Bernstein conference. 

Returning the 737 to a higher — and steady — production rate is critical to monetising Boeing’s backlog of 5,600 unfilled jet orders and reversing years of financial losses. The company has been working for months to boost the tempo of its factories and bolster quality after years of tumult and delays.

Boeing’s shares rose as much as 5.4% during Ortberg’s remarks, extending a 14% gain by the stock this year through Wednesday’s close.

Boeing is already “pulsing” parts of its 737 assembly lines to test its ability to produce aircraft at rates higher than the FAA’s cap, and the results so far are encouraging, Ortberg said. Suppliers have also kept pace with the sharp step up in output.

Longer term, Boeing is laying plans to eventually produce more than 60 of its narrow-body MAX a month.

Uploaded by Felyx Teoh

data.title: MACC seizes assets worth RM32m belonging to ‘Tan Sri’ in highway funds probe
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (May 29): The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) seized various assets worth RM32 million believed to belong to a highway concessionnaire with the title ‘Tan Sri’, following an investigation into the misappropriation of highway construction sukuk funds in the Klang Valley on Thursday.

According to a source, among the items seized were handbags, jewellery, luxury vehicles, watches and cash after the MACC Investigation Division searched two residences belonging to the individual in the capital.

The source stated that in the search, MACC seized 217 luxury watches of various brands estimated to be worth about RM5 million, 27 handbags worth over RM1 million and jewellery valued at RM4 million.

“During the search, MACC also seized nine luxury vehicles such as Bentley, Mercedes-Benz and Range Rover worth almost RM7 million,” it said.

The source added that following the search, MACC also found a storage containing various types of luxury liquor in another residence estimated to be worth about RM3 million and believed to be linked to money-laundering activities.

“In the same operation, MACC also seized another luxury residential unit in the capital estimated to be worth around RM12 million, which is believed to belong to the individual," he said.

The source also explained that all goods and assets were seized for the purpose of further investigation under the MACC Act 2009 and the Anti-Money Laundering and Anti-Terrorism Financing Act (AMLA) 2001.

Uploaded by Magessan Varatharaja

data.title: Barakah to be delisted on June 4, says not appealing Bursa's decision
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (May 29): Oil and gas company Barakah Offshore Petroleum Bhd (KL:BARAKAH) said it will not appeal against its impending delisting from Bursa Malaysia, which will take effect on June 4.

In its filing, the Practice Note 17 (PN17) company, which is in a net-cash position at end-March, said it has "exhausted all available avenues" to regularise its financial condition and operations.

“The company has decided not to submit an appeal to Bursa against the delisting within the appeal timeframe (by May 29),” it said.

The announcement to the stock exchange was made by Malacca Securities Sdn Bhd on behalf of the company on Thursday.

On May 22, the company announced that its shares would be suspended from trading on May 30 and may be delisted on June 4, after it failed to obtain additional time to submit a regularisation plan.

With the appeal window closing and no submission made, the delisting will proceed following Bursa Securities’ rules.

Barakah, which was listed in the Main Market in November 2013, triggered PN17 status in March 2019 after it defaulted on payments to EXIM Bank for a vessel.

In its nine months ended March (9MFY2025), Barakah posted a net profit of RM32.56 million versus a RM4.84 million loss in the same period last year. Revenue nearly halved to RM52.78 million from RM97.05 million.

The group had cash of RM88.06 million against borrowings of RM48.75 million at end-March, for a net cash position of RM39.31 million.

Shares in Barakah ended unchanged at half a sen on Thursday, giving the company a market capitalisation of RM2.51 million.

Read also:
Barakah to be suspended, faces delisting after Bursa rejects regularisation plan extension

Edited ByAdam Aziz

data.title: Cocoa extends drop on US tariff ruling, improved crop prospects
data.description:
(May 29): Cocoa hit its longest losing streak in nearly three years after the US trade court’s decision to block the vast majority of President Donald Trump’s global tariffs added to a bearish momentum.

The ruling deemed illegal the bulk of Trump’s levies, including the global flat tariff that had made it costly for chocolate companies in the US to import cocoa. The New York contract’s premium over London futures for July delivery narrowed to about US$770 (RM3,256) a tonne, down from US$875 earlier this week.

The most-active contract in New York fell as much as 6% to US$9,060 a tonne, and headed for the longest run of declines since July 2022. That’s seen futures trim their monthly advance to about 3%, despite soaring past US$11,000 a tonne earlier this month on supply fears. 

Weather in West Africa — where the bulk of the global cocoa supplies are sourced — has improved following widespread rains, easing concerns about the next harvest, according to Steve Wateridge, head of research at Tropical Research Services by Expana.

“There’s been some uncertainty about the weather and the impact on the crops but things are looking better than they were a month ago,” he said. Still, “we are destroying so much demand and there’s a strong argument that the market at the moment is overvalued unless we get poor crops”.

Last week, Asia’s top processor, Guan Chong Bhd said grinders are struggling to maintain capacity and smaller players are getting pushed out of the industry due to high prices. 

Traders are also waiting for the International Cocoa Organisation’s next report, expected later this week, for more direction on this season’s supply outlook. 

Prices:

  • Cocoa futures fell 4.8% to US$9,168 a tonne in New York. London cocoa fell 4.1%
  • Raw sugar rose 0.6% to 17 cents a pound

Uploaded by Felyx Teoh

data.title: Wall Street gains after court blocks most Trump tariffs
data.description:
(May 29): Wall Street's main indices rose on Thursday after a federal court ruled against most of US President Donald Trump's tariffs and artificial intelligence (AI) bellwether Nvidia reported a 69% surge in quarterly sales.

Nvidia jumped 5% after reporting higher-than-expected quarterly sales growth, driven by customers stockpiling AI chips ahead of US export restrictions on China.

The company, however, warned that the new curbs are expected to cut US$8 billion (RM33.83 billion) from its current-quarter sales.

"We're in a secular growth trajectory here for AI, AI investment, and everything seems to be lining up for the next kind of multi-quarter expansion across AI," said David Russell, global head of market strategy at TradeStation.

The Philadelphia SE Semiconductor index rose in the wake of Nvidia's results, last up 1.4%.

Eight of the 11 major S&P 500 sub-sectors rose, with information technology and consumer discretionary  being the biggest gainers.

Global risk appetite was boosted after a US court invalidated with immediate effect most of Trump's sweeping levies imposed since January, but did not address some industry-specific tariffs on automobiles, steel and aluminum.

"Even if the ruling is upheld, the administration will have alternative routes to implement tariffs. But these will be slower and more targeted, as opposed to the current sweeping approach," said Lizzy Galbraith, senior political economist at Aberdeen.

Kevin Hassett, the White House economic adviser, said three trade deals were nearly done and he expected more despite the judgment.

At 9.47am ET, the Dow Jones Industrial Average rose 51.16 points, or 0.12%, to 42,149.86, the S&P 500 gained 32.6 points, or 0.55%, to 5,921.01 and the Nasdaq Composite gained 173.41 points, or 0.91%, to 19,273.71.

Dow component Salesforce weighed on the index, with its shares slumping 5.4% even as the enterprise software provider raised its annual revenue and adjusted profit forecasts.

Apple, which Trump threatened with tariffs last week, rose nearly 1%. Tesla rose 2.2% and Amazon added 1.2% among megacap and growth stocks.

The S&P 500 is currently about 3% below an all-time high touched on Feb 19, rebounding from a nearly 19% decline earlier in April on easing trade tensions, strong earnings and subdued inflation data that aided risk appetite.

May has been a solid month for equities, with both the S&P 500 and the Nasdaq on pace for their best monthly performances since November 2023.

A second reading from the Commerce Department showed gross domestic product contracted 0.2% in the first quarter. Economists polled by Reuters had forecast a 0.3% contraction.

At least five Fed policymakers including Fed Board governor Adriana Kugler are scheduled to speak through the day.

In other earnings, Best Buy dropped 7.5% after the electronics retailer lowered its annual comparable sales and profit forecasts amid concerns that US tariffs would weigh on consumer demand for big-ticket items.

Advancing issues outnumbered decliners by a 2.63-to-1 ratio on the NYSE and by a 1.73-to-1 ratio on the Nasdaq.

The S&P 500 posted five new 52-week highs and no new lows, while the Nasdaq Composite recorded 25 new highs and 23 new lows.

Uploaded by Felyx Teoh

data.title: Vestland secures RM157 mil apartment project in KL
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (May 29): Construction outfit Vestland Bhd (KL:VLB) on Thursday secured a RM156.91 million contract to undertake the construction of a 51-storey serviced apartment block in Kuala Lumpur.

The contract, which is the first win for the company in 2025, was awarded by Mercu Majuniaga Sdn Bhd, said Vestland in a bourse filing.

Work at the site located in Lorong Raja Uda 2 is scheduled to be completed within 26 months, by July 31, 2027.

Vestland said the contract is anticipated to positively contribute to the company’s earnings and net assets during the contract period.

Shares in Vestland closed one sen or 2.27% lower to 43 sen on Thursday, valuing the ACE Market company at RM406.05 million. The counter has dropped over 23% year-to-date.

Edited ByS Kanagaraju

data.title: KPJ's 1Q profit falls 20% on losses overseas, pays another dividend
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (May 29): Private hospital operator KPJ Healthcare Bhd’s (KL:KPJ) net profit fell 20% year-on-year (y-o-y) in the first quarter of 2025, dragged by losses in its overseas operations, coupled with the absence of discontinued operations’ contribution in Australia.

Net profit for the three months ended March 31, 2025 (1QFY2025) was RM57.06 million, against RM71.39 million in the same quarter last year, the group said in a filing on Thursday. Earnings per share dropped to 1.31 sen from 1.63 sen a year ago.

Revenue for the quarter, meanwhile, rose 7% to RM971.8 million from RM908.02 million, supported by increased patient visits and bed capacity at its Malaysian hospitals.

Malaysia contributed 98% of the group's revenue, with a 7% increase y-o-y to RM955.4 million in revenue, as compared to RM892.5 million a year ago. 

KPJ is also involved in the provision of hospital and retirement village services in Thailand, Bangladesh, and Australia. This segment recorded a loss before tax of RM3.4 million, while revenue rose 6% to RM17.1 million.

KPJ, in which Johor's largest enterprise Johor Corp (JCorp) owns a 44.99% stake, declared an interim dividend of 0.8 sen per share, amounting to RM34.9 million, payable on July 11, with an ex-date of June 20. The group has so far declared a total dividend of 1.95 sen per share in FY2025, following an earlier interim dividend payout of 1.05 sen per share and a special dividend of 0.1 sen per share.

Compared with the immediate preceding quarter, KPJ’s net profit declined 52.7% from RM120.52 million in 4QFY2024, as revenue fell 7.5% from RM1.05 billion previously.

“The group remains cautiously optimistic about its prospects for FY2025, bolstered by its ongoing asset optimisation programme, capacity expansion and efforts to enhance operational efficiency,” it said.

Shares in KPJ settled down two sen or 0.67% to RM2.96 on Thursday, valuing the group at RM13.4 billion. The counter has risen over 25% since the beginning of this year.

Edited ByAdam Aziz

data.title: Nvidia faces bipartisan concern on China research facility plans
data.description:
(May 29): Republican Senator Jim Banks and Democrat Elizabeth Warren are demanding answers from Nvidia Corp CEO Jensen Huang on his firm’s reported plans to open a research facility in Shanghai, saying the decision “raises significant national security and economic security concerns.”

Banks and Warren were reacting to a Financial Times report that Nvidia is opening a research and development centre in the Chinese city. The news organisation, citing people with knowledge of the matter, said the facility would be used for a potential expansion as well as existing employees and that the company is advertising to hire artificial intelligence talent in China.

Nvidia spokesperson Sarah Weinstein said in an emailed statement that the company “is simply leasing a new space for existing employees, who need the room in the post-Covid return to work.”

“The scope of work will remain unchanged,” she added.

The two senators warned the centre could result in sensitive information being leaked, stolen or otherwise transferred to China.

Warren and Banks said Nvidia must consider how use of its products could impact US national security. They said the company’s innovation should “occur within the sphere of close democratic allies who share our values.”

“Nvidia’s great financial and technology success was built on American innovation, American university research funded by taxpayers, and the capital, security and freedom we provide — not through collaboration with autocracies,” they wrote.

They argued such a move would erode trust between the company and Congress and create a “talent pipeline” for China, citing the report that Nvidia is advertising in the country for engineers. The senators asked the company to provide detailed information about the plans by June 20.

Uploaded by Magessan Varatharaja

data.title: New York Times agrees to license content to Amazon for AI use
data.description:
(May 29): The New York Times Co has reached a deal to license its editorial content to Amazon.com Inc for use across its artificial intelligence platforms, marking the first such agreement the newspaper company has struck after years of battles between the media and a new, disruptive technology.

The multi-year collaboration “will bring Times editorial content to a variety of Amazon customer experiences,” the news outlet said in a statement Thursday. In addition to editorial content, the deal will include recipes from NYT Cooking and The Athletic, which focuses on sports.

“This broadens the companies’ existing relationship, and will bring additional value to Amazon customers and bring Times journalism to wider audiences,” the publisher said.

Summaries and short excerpts of Times content will be displayed within Amazon products and services, such as the Alexa voice platform, and used to train Amazon’s proprietary AI models, according to the statement.

Terms of the deal weren’t disclosed, and Amazon declined to comment.

The New York Times remains embroiled in a lawsuit with OpenAI and its partner Microsoft Corp over copyright infringement. The Times sued the tech companies in 2023, accusing them of using millions of articles published by the newspaper to train chatbots without compensation.

OpenAI has been accused of scraping text across the web to train its popular ChatGPT chatbot since it debuted in 2022. The AI start-up has been sued by prominent authors and other news publishers for breach of copyright. The narrative-style answers that AI tools like ChatGPT provide rival, and sometimes replicate, news articles, which has caused publishers concern about losing readers and advertising revenue. Dozens of publishers have signed lucrative licensing deals with OpenAI, Alphabet Inc and Perplexity AI in order to wrest back some of that lost business.

Uploaded by Magessan Varatharaja

data.title: Global air cargo demand up by 5.8% y-o-y in April — IATA
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (May 29): The International Air Transport Association (IATA) has reported a year-on-year (y-o-y) increase in global air cargo demand for April 2025.

Total demand, measured in cargo tonne-kilometres (CTK), rose by 5.8% in volume, building on March's solid performance, IATA said in a statement on Thursday.

International operations saw an even stronger increase of 6.5%, while in terms of capacity, measured in available cargo tonne-kilometres (ACTK), ticked up by 6.3% globally and 6.9% for international routes.

In April 2024, the total demand for international operations was 6.5% higher. In terms of capacity, measured in ACTK, increased by 6.3%, compared with April 2024 of 6.9% for international operations. 

IATA director general Willie Walsh said seasonal demand for fashion and consumer goods, front-loading ahead of the US tariff changes, and lower jet fuel prices have combined to boost air cargo. 

"With available capacity at record levels and yields improving, the outlook for air cargo is encouraging. While April brought good news, stresses in world trade are no secret. 

"Shifts in trade policy, particularly in the US, are already reshaping demand and export dynamics. Airlines will need to remain flexible as the situation develops over the coming months," he said. 

Several economic indicators supported the uptick in air cargo performance, including the increase in global industrial production, which rose 3.2% y-o-y in March.

It also noted that jet fuel prices declined by 21.2% from the previous year and 4.1% compared with March, marking the third consecutive month of falling fuel costs.

Besides, global manufacturing Purchasing Managers’ Index (PMI) rose to 50.5 in April, signalling continued expansion for the fourth month in a row.

"However, there are also signs of caution. The PMI for new export orders dropped 2.8 points to 47.2, remaining below the 50-point benchmark that indicates growth," it said. 

In terms of regional performance in April, Asia-Pacific airlines saw 10% y-o-y demand growth for air cargo, while capacity increased by 9.4% y-o-y. 

On trade lane growth, IATA said all international routes experienced growth in April, except for Middle East-Europe, Africa-Asia, and intra-European routes.

Overall, the data reflect a robust air cargo sector buoyed by economic and seasonal tailwinds, though the potential for policy-driven shifts remains a key risk in the months ahead.

The IATA represents some 350 airlines comprising over 80 per cent of global air traffic.

In terms of total cargo traffic measured by CTK, the Asia-Pacific region holds the largest market share at 34.2%. North America follows with 25.8%, while Europe accounts for 21.5% of the global share.

The Middle East contributes 13.6% to the total cargo traffic, whereas Latin America and Africa represent smaller portions, with 2.9% and 2%, respectively. These figures highlight the dominant role of Asia-Pacific, North America, and Europe in global air cargo movement.

Uploaded by Felyx Teoh

data.title: Musk's Trump administration exit lifts Tesla shares on hopes of renewed focus
data.description:
(May 29): Tesla shares rose more than 2% on Thursday after news that CEO Elon Musk was stepping away from the Trump administration, fuelling hopes that he will sharpen focus on the automaker as it gears up for its hotly anticipated robotaxi debut.

Musk's ties with US President Donald Trump and his tilt toward right-wing politics in Europe had fueled waves of protests against Tesla in recent months, pushing some buyers away and rattling investors worried about brand damage.

With Tesla on track for a second straight annual sales decline after its first-ever drop in 2024, pressure was mounting on the world's richest man — who also owns SpaceX and X — to refocus on the automaker that underpins much of his fortune.

Musk signalled recently he was cutting back on his role with the Department of Government Efficiency, telling shareholders in March he would reduce his time to a day or two per week.

He also announced last week that he will cut his political spending substantially. Musk spent nearly US$300 million (RM1.2 billion) to back Trump's presidential campaign and other Republicans last year.

Musk's departure from the administration "is music to the ears of Tesla shareholders," said Wedbush Securities analyst Dan Ives, a long-time Tesla bull who had last month called the Tesla chief's time in Washington a "code red situation" that could create lasting demand destruction.

"2025 started off as a dark chapter for Musk and Tesla," he added, "but importantly those days are in the rear-view mirror."

Tesla shares, trading at US$363 before the bell, have declined 11% this year.

News of Musk's departure was shortly followed by a post on X from Musk announcing that Tesla has been testing driverless Model Y cars in Austin, Texas, with no incidents and aims to deliver the first unit in June.

The roll-out is crucial for Tesla as Musk has shifted the company's focus away from building a new, cheaper electric vehicle platform to launching the robotaxi service and its Optimus humanoid robots. Much of Tesla's valuation hangs on that bet.

"Musk's departure from DOGE will improve market sentiment for Tesla, but I see no real change for Tesla," Morningstar analyst Seth Goldstein said.

Uploaded by Magessan Varatharaja

data.title: AmanahRaya REIT to buy industrial asset in Selangor for RM39m
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (May 29): Amanahraya Real Estate Investment Trust (AmanahRaya REIT) (KL:ARREIT) is acquiring an industrial building, measuring 132,700 sq ft, in Telok Panglima Garang, Selangor for RM39 million.

Pacific Trustees Bhd, acting on behalf AmanahRaya REIT, entered into an agreement on Thursday to buy the property from Alpha Express Sdn Bhd, said AmanahRaya REIT in an exchange filing.

It noted that the RM39 million price tag reflects a slight discount from the property’s RM40 million market valuation as of May 2.

Upon completion, the property will be leased back to the seller, Alpha Express, under a 10-year triple net lease at RM195,000 per month, subject to rent reversion for each renewal term.

“The proposed acquisition of the property will be accretive to AmanahRaya REIT’s distributable income and adds to the portfolio of properties that will benefit the fund in the long term,” said the REIT, which is 45.6%-owned by Minister of Finance Inc trustee company Amanah Raya Bhd.

AmanahRaya REIT noted that the property is located near the South Klang Valley Expressway and the West Coast Expressway.

The REIT said it will fund the purchase via bank borrowings. As at end-March 2025, its cash stood at RM24.63 million, while borrowings totalled RM571.88 million.

The transaction is expected to close by the fourth quarter of 2025, it added.

Shares in AmanahRaya REIT closed up one sen or 2.67% to 38.5 sen on Thursday, valuing the trust at RM220.70 million.

Edited ByS Kanagaraju

data.title: US corporate profits fell by most since 2020 ahead of tariffs
data.description:
(May 29): US corporate profits fell in the first quarter by the most since 2020, indicating large companies were already feeling some pressure prior to the Trump administration’s sweeping tariffs on global trading partners.

The 2.9% decrease in profits followed a 5.4% advance in the fourth quarter, according to Bureau of Economic Analysis data out Thursday. Despite the drop, profits remained well above historical norms relative to gross domestic product, which fell 0.2%.

The extent to which American companies choose to raise consumer prices in response to tariffs on imported goods has become a key question for the US economic outlook in 2025. Wall Street economists generally expect inflation to pick up this year, hitting disposable household incomes and undercutting growth.

President Donald Trump recently singled out Walmart Inc in a social media post after the retail giant warned of coming price increases. The president highlighted the company’s profits, arguing it should “‘EAT THE TARIFFS,’ and not charge valued customers ANYTHING.”

Details of Thursday’s report showed a measure of after-tax profits for nonfinancial firms as a share of gross value added — a proxy for margins — edged down to 15.7% from 15.9%, remaining well above levels that prevailed from 1951 to 2019. The modest decline suggests large companies still have some room to absorb higher costs from tariffs without passing those on to consumers.

Uploaded by Chng Shear Lane

data.title: Wasco eyes recurring income stream from biomass plant ownership, says CEO
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (May 29): Wasco Bhd (KL:WASCO), which specialises in oil and gas (O&G) pipes, is looking to cultivate recurring income from its biomass power plants.

Managing director and group chief executive officer Giancarlo Maccagno said the group intends to deepen its footprint in the bioenergy segment by evolving from a pure engineering, procurement, construction, and commissioning (EPCC) player to an asset owner.

“The plan is to move beyond just building plants for others. If we can lock in long-term feedstock supply and secure offtake agreements — for example selling energy to Tenaga Nasional (KL:TENAGA) — we could create a stable, recurring income stream,” Maccagno told reporters after its 25th annual general meeting.

“This is a natural extension of our core competencies and highly complementary to our existing renewable energy business,” he added.

Wasco currently undertakes full EPCC scope for biomass projects, including the supply of steam turbines, mainly catering to the palm oil sector. The group sees strong prospects in Malaysia and Indonesia, where empty fruit bunches (EFB) from palm oil mills are an underutilised source of renewable energy.

Maccagno, however, was quick to point out that the group would only proceed when both supply and demand sides have been secured.

"The risk lies in the feedstock provider walking away or the buyer defaulting. We will not rush into it until all the boxes are ticked,” he said, noting project costs could range between RM30 million and RM40 million per plant.

The group is not committing to a timeline for its maiden investment, preferring to wait for the right opportunity. “We are not under pressure. Our current business is doing well. But this biomass strategy represents a very exciting future because it allows us to reduce the project-based volatility in our revenue,” he added.

The push into biomass aligns with Malaysia’s evolving energy policy, which is increasingly supportive of renewable sources beyond solar, including biomass and small hydro.

As at end-FY2024, Wasco held a group order book of RM2.5 billion, with 85% derived from the O&G segment and the remaining 15% from renewables. Its tender book currently stands at RM13 billion.

Read also:
Wasco CEO warns of lower earnings in FY2025 due to project delays from market uncertainties

Edited ByKathy Fong

data.title: Dagang NeXchange swings to loss in 1Q on higher interest expense
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (May 29): Dagang NeXchange Bhd (KL:DNEX) has posted a net loss of RM79.04 million for the first quarter, compared with a net profit of RM14.46 million a year earlier, mainly due to a higher interest expense.

The company recorded an income tax expense of RM115.65 million for the three months ended March 31, 2025 (1QFY2025), against an income tax credit of RM1.43 million previously. Deferred tax liabilities rose to RM868.16 million from RM766.10 million.

The increase in deferred tax liabilities was mainly due to the UK Energy Profits Levy (EPL), after a bill extending the levy from March 31, 2028 to March 31, 2030, was effectively passed on March 3, said DNeX in its bourse filing on Thursday.

In comparison, the same quarter last year saw a reversal of deferred tax liabilities from remeasuring EPL-related temporary differences.

The EPL was first introduced by the UK government in July 2022 as a temporary tax of 25% on profits from upstream oil and gas activities. The rate was later raised to 35%, starting from 2023 until March 31, 2028. On July 29, 2024, the government announced another increase, raising the EPL rate to 38%, effective from Nov 1, 2024.

DNeX's 90%-owned unit Ping Petroleum wholly owns UK-based upstream oil and gas firm Ping Petroleum UK plc.

DNeX did not declare any dividend for 1QFY2025.

The company's quarterly revenue fell 4.19% to RM296.83 million from RM309.82 million in 1QFY2024, mainly due to weaker contributions from the energy and information technology segments, which offset gains in the technology segment.

Revenue from the technology segment rose 30% to RM178.71 million from RM137.96 million a year ago, driven by an improved product mix and stronger market demand. This was further supported by growing demand for emerging technologies, which made up 37% of the product mix, compared to 19% a year earlier.

The average selling price per wafer rose significantly to US$589 (RM2,490), up from US$467 a year ago. Wafer shipments also increased to 63,400 units, compared to 56,600 units in 1QFY2024.

Revenue from the energy segment fell 26% to RM78.81 million from RM105.93 million in 1QFY2024, mainly due to lower average crude oil prices and slightly reduced lifting volumes.

Crude oil was sold at US$74.30 per barrel in 1QFY2025, down 15% from US$87.70 in the same quarter last year. Lifting volume also fell 7% year-on-year to 175,000 barrels, further weighing on revenue performance.

Revenue from the information technology segment dropped 40% to RM39.31 million, primarily due to the absence of contributions from the subsea telco business following its divestment.

Looking ahead to 2025, the company said its 60%-owned unit Silterra Malaysia Sdn Bhd is expanding its capabilities in silicon photonics, an enabling technology for high-speed, energy-efficient data transmission in hyperscale data centres. This innovation is crucial for next-generation AI and cloud computing applications, providing faster data processing with significantly lower power consumption.

"SilTerra is experiencing growing demand for technologies such as MEMS on CMOS, Life Sciences, and Smart Power — serving critical use cases in smart devices, biomedical instrumentation, electric vehicles, and renewable energy systems," the company added.

For its energy segment, the company said Ping continues to operate the producing Anasuria field while planning the development of greenfield assets — Fyne, Avalon, and Pilot — alongside a rejuvenation plan for its brownfield Hutton asset.

Despite a softer crude oil price environment, DNeX's disciplined cost structure and focus on production efficiency are expected to maintain profitability, the company said.

For its information technology segment, DNeX said it will continue to expand its business beyond government-driven projects to include more private sector digital transformation initiatives.

Shares of DNeX closed flat at 29.5 sen, giving the company a market capitalisation of RM1.02 billion. The counter has declined 26.25% so far this year.

Edited ByS Kanagaraju
